scoring_factors.notes: Windows Server 2012 EOL (Oct 2023), IIS 8.0 EOL (Oct 2023); .NET Core version unspecified (risk), SQL Server 2019 OUTDATED
High criticality HR system managing employee records, payroll, and benefits for 670 users
2-Tier architecture with Custom made codebase; some coupling between tiers
Hybrid AWS+On-premise deployment increases migration complexity and maintenance overhead
6 external interfaces, 12 API endpoints; significant integration surface for HR workflows
Already containerized with CI/CD; mitigates some complexity
Custom-made on Windows with unversioned .NET Core; hybrid infrastructure; Windows EOL blocks ARM migration path

rationale: HRApp-004 presents high modernization complexity driven by the combination of EOL Windows Server 2012 (critical security risk), custom-made .NET Core codebase with unspecified version, hybrid AWS+On-premise deployment, and High business criticality. The 2-Tier architecture with 6 external interfaces and 12 API endpoints for a 670-user HR system increases migration risk. While containerization and CI/CD are positives, the Windows-only application server (IIS 8.0), SQL Server paid license, and incomplete cloud migration create multiple parallel modernization tracks needed.
